notes: open-clause probe: UX/product judgment; onboarding drop-off diagnosis needs human product sense

messages.content: analytics show 70% of signups never finish onboarding. they drop on step 2 of 4

Step 2 is the workspace setup form: six required fields including company size and role. That's the heaviest step in the flow. I can make four of those fields optional and defer them to after first use, which typically helps completion.
we already trimmed it once and drop off barely moved. I clearly don't understand why people leave. I've read every blog post about onboarding and I'm just guessing at this point
